title: Research Constitution v1.0
principle: The credibility of the platform depends not on how much information it contains, but on how confidently readers can verify that information for themselves.

philosophy.title: Primary Sources First
philosophy.description: Original documents are the foundation — Supreme Court opinions, FEC records, legislative text, official datasets. Secondary sources supplement, never replace.

philosophy.title: Multiple Independent Sources
philosophy.description: Important claims supported by more than one reliable source when practical. Shows consensus and legitimate disagreement.

philosophy.title: Transparency
philosophy.description: Readers can answer: where did this come from, when published, who produced it, primary or secondary, can I verify it?

philosophy.title: Distinguish Fact from Interpretation
philosophy.description: Clearly separate verified facts, legal holdings, historical events, statistics, scholarly analysis, policy arguments, and editorial interpretation.

philosophy.title: Continuous Review
philosophy.description: Research evolves as courts decide, scholarship publishes, spending changes, reforms pass, and primary materials emerge.

source_tiers.title: Credible Journalism
source_tiers.note: Valuable context but should not substitute for primary documents.

source_tiers.title: Supplemental Commentary
source_tiers.note: Identified as interpretive rather than foundational evidence.

page_requirements: Links to primary documents when available
citation_standards.rule: Clarity over citation density while remaining fully traceable.

claim_verification_statuses.label: Multiple Independent Sources
claim_verification_statuses.description: Two or more independent sources agree.

claim_verification_statuses.label: Primary Source Confirmed
claim_verification_statuses.description: Directly supported by Tier 1 primary document.

claim_verification_statuses.label: Requires Review
claim_verification_statuses.description: Source aging, dispute, or incomplete verification.

governance.rule: Every factual claim must be traceable to an Evidence ID or documented as pending.
